commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=1eae67ec6f12d56d5442c019621c5ebf7250d458)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a `not supported model` regex to `src/agents/live-model-errors.ts` so matching provider error text can classify as `model_not_found`.

PR surface: Source +3. Total +3 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main lacks the `not supported model` matcher, and body-bearing 400/422 responses fall back to `format` when no message classifier matches. I did not run a live provider request.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed for APIError-param handling, committed regression tests, and redacted real-runtime proof; ClawSweeper should not replace the PR because the proof must come from the contributor or a maintainer-run live setup.

target.title: fix(agents): handle "Not supported model" error format in isModelNotFoundErrorMessage
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92118

`isModelNotFoundErrorMessage()` in `src/agents/live-model-errors.ts` did not recognize the `"Not supported model <model-id>"` error format returned by some OpenAI-compatible provider gateways (e.g. Xiaomi MiFY and similar Chinese cloud proxies).

This caused the error to fall through to `isSchemaErrorMessage()` and be misclassified as `"schema"`, showing the user a misleading message about tool payload rejection instead of a model configuration issue.

### Change

Added one pattern to `isModelNotFoundErrorMessage()`:

```typescript
if (/not supported model/i.test(msg)) {
  return true;
}
```

The regex is case-insensitive, unambiguous, and has no false-positive risk against other error types.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `isModelNotFoundErrorMessage` returned `false` for errors with message `"Param Incorrect … Not supported model some-model-id"`, causing misclassification as a schema error
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 11, Node.js, source-level unit test against `live-model-errors.ts`
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```
  npx vitest run --project agents src/agents/live-model-errors
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```
  ✓ src/agents/live-model-errors.test.ts
    ✓ isModelNotFoundErrorMessage
      ✓ returns true for "Not supported model gpt-4o"
      ✓ returns true for "not supported model some-model-id"
      ✓ existing patterns still match
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** Error correctly classified as `model_not_found`; failover and user-facing messaging behave as expected
- **What was not tested:** Live request against a real provider gateway that returns this exact format
